chunk_summary: Guidelines for transitioning from stabilization to rehabilitation feeding in children with stunting and severe acute malnutrition, emphasizing safe catch-up weight gain, discharge follow-up, and prevention of relapse.
learning_objectives: Understand the staged approach to nutritional rehabilitation for children with stunting and severe acute malnutrition.
Learn discharge follow-up criteria and schedules to prevent relapse and ensure sustained recovery.
Identify high-risk failure patterns such as stalled weight gain or recurring edema.
key_facts: Rehabilitation feeding transitions from a starter diet to catch-up feeding only after clinical stabilization, appetite return, and edema reduction.
Energy intake during rehabilitation should not exceed 220 kcal/kg/day to prevent complications.
Follow-up schedule: one week post-discharge, every two weeks for the first month, then monthly until anthropometric recovery thresholds are met.
Discharge phenotype, feeding regimen, and medications must be documented for outpatient continuity.
Relapse or stalled weight gain should prompt re-evaluation and possible re-admission rather than calorie escalation at home.
reference_quote: Energy is increased gradually with frequent feeds, and many protocols specify an upper ceiling around 220 kcal/kg/day during rehabilitation to prevent complications. (WHO guideline)
document_key_topics: Stunting and severe acute malnutrition coexistence management
